text.title: DALL-E 3 Generation and Batch Patterns
text.summary: generate_image and generate_and_save functions; DALL-E 2 variation and edit endpoints; BatchImageGenerator with ThreadPoolExecutor.

section.title: DALL-E 3 Basic Generation
section.p: DALL-E 3 is limited to n=1 per call and silently rewrites prompts. Always log revised_prompt alongside the original for audit purposes.

example.code: def generate_image(prompt: str, size: str = "1024x1024", quality: str = "standard", style: str = "vivid") -> dict: """ size: "1024x1024" | "1792x1024" | "1024x1792" quality: "standard" | "hd" style: "vivid" (creative) | "natural" (photorealistic) """ response = client.images.generate( model="dall-e-3", prompt=prompt, size=size, quality=quality, style=style, n=1 ) return { "url": response.data[0].url, "revised_prompt": response.data[0].revised_prompt # always log this }
example.note: Download the image immediately — DALL-E URLs are pre-signed and expire in ~1 hour.

rule.statement: Always download and store generated images immediately. Never rely on DALL-E provider URLs as permanent storage.
rule.rationale: DALL-E pre-signed URLs expire in approximately 60 minutes. Pipelines that reference URLs hours later receive 404 responses.

section.title: DALL-E 2 Endpoints
section.p: DALL-E 2 supports image variations and inpainting edits. These endpoints require PNG images with an alpha channel — passing JPEG causes a cryptic 400 error.

antipattern.description: Building new workflows on DALL-E 2 variation or edit endpoints.
antipattern.reason: These endpoints are being deprecated. New workflows should use DALL-E 3 or Flux for generation and GPT-4o Vision for variation via the describe-then-generate cycle.

section.title: Batch Generation
section.p: BatchImageGenerator uses ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent calls. DALL-E 3 rate limit is 5 images/minute (12s/image) on tier 1 — threads will 429 without rate limiting.

antipattern.description: Running ThreadPoolExecutor with 10 threads on a DALL-E tier-1 API key.
antipattern.reason: DALL-E 3 rate limit is per-organization not per-key. 10 concurrent threads will immediately exhaust the 5 img/min limit and fail with 429.
